name: @playwright/test
summary: End-to-end browser testing framework that runs tests in Chromium, Firefox, and WebKit.

story.problem: Unit tests pass but users still hit broken flows. The checkout button works in isolation but breaks when combined with auth state and the cart. You need tests that run in a real browser, click real buttons, and verify what users actually see.
story.mental_model: Playwright controls a real browser (Chromium, Firefox, or WebKit) via the DevTools protocol. You navigate pages, interact with elements using locators (resilient selectors), and assert on page state. Tests run in parallel across browsers. Failed tests auto-save screenshots and traces.
story.when_to_use: Critical user flows (login, checkout, onboarding), cross-browser compatibility checks, visual regression testing, scraping JS-rendered content, accessibility audits.
story.when_not_to_use: Unit or integration testing (Vitest/Jest is faster). Tests that don't need a real browser. Simple API testing (use supertest or fetch).

alternatives.name: cypress
alternatives.reason: Better DX, interactive test runner, but Chromium-only and slower on CI.

alternatives.name: puppeteer
alternatives.reason: Google's CDP library, Chromium-only, no test runner built in.

alternatives.name: selenium
alternatives.reason: Mature, multi-language, but verbose API and slower than Playwright.

nodes.label: @playwright/test
nodes.summary: Import test, expect, Browser, Page from '@playwright/test'.

nodes.label: test(name, fn)
nodes.summary: `test('login flow', async ({ page }) => {...})`. Page fixture is auto-provided.

nodes.label: Page
nodes.summary: Represents a browser tab. page.goto(), page.click(), page.fill(), page.screenshot(), page.waitFor*.

nodes.label: Locator
nodes.summary: Resilient element selector. page.getByRole(), getByText(), getByLabel(), getByTestId(), locator(css).

nodes.label: Fixtures
nodes.summary: test.extend() to create custom fixtures (authenticated page, DB state). Shared across tests.

nodes.label: playwright.config.ts
nodes.summary: Define projects (browser/device matrix), baseURL, retries, reporter, use options.

nodes.label: Trace Viewer
nodes.summary: `--trace on` records a trace on failure. `npx playwright show-trace` shows step-by-step with screenshots.

steps.label: Write test
Run
steps.code:
npx playwright test
npx playwright test --ui  # interactive mode

steps.label: Fill and submit
steps.code:
test('login', async ({ page }) => {
  await page.goto('/login')
  await page.getByLabel('Email').fill('user@example.com')
  await page.getByLabel('Password').fill('secret')
  await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Sign in' }).click()
  await expect(page).toHaveURL('/dashboard')
})

steps.label: Fixture
Use fixture
